- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""
2	Live monitoring server for POLARIS pipeline.
3	
4	FastAPI application serving a live dashboard via HTTP, streaming pipeline
5	trace events via SSE, and optionally spawning a Cloudflare Quick Tunnel
6	for remote access.

8	Endpoints:
9	    GET  /                  Serve live_dashboard.html
10	    GET  /health            Health check with uptime and pipeline status

12	    GET  /api/snapshot       All events grouped by type + computed stats
13	    GET  /api/anomalies      Contents of logs/live_anomaly_log.jsonl
14	    GET  /api/cost           Cost ledger entries for current session
15	    POST /api/research       Start a new research pipeline run
16	    GET  /api/research/status Current research status
17	    POST /api/research/cancel Cancel running research
18	    GET  /api/research/history  List all completed research results
19	    POST /api/research/export/{vector_id}  Export result as PDF/HTML
20	    POST /api/campaigns            Create a new research campaign
21	    GET  /api/campaigns            List all campaigns
22	    GET  /api/campaigns/{id}       Get campaign details with per-query status
23	    POST /api/campaigns/{id}/start Start executing a campaign

25	    POST /api/documents/upload       Upload a document for local RAG

393	class InMemoryRateLimiter:
394	    """Simple in-memory rate limiter as fallback when slowapi is not installed.
395	
396	    Tracks IP -> last_request_timestamp for a single endpoint.
397	    Thread-safe via asyncio (single-threaded event loop).
398	    """
399	
400	    def __init__(self, window_seconds: int = 60):
401	        self._window = window_seconds
402	        self._requests: dict[str, float] = {}
403	
404	    def check(self, client_ip: str) -> bool:
405	        """Return True if request is allowed, False if rate-limited."""
406	        now = time.time()
407	        last = self._requests.get(client_ip, 0.0)
408	        if now - last < self._window:
409	            return False
410	        self._requests[client_ip] = now
411	        # Periodic cleanup: remove entries older than 2x window
412	        if len(self._requests) > 1000:
413	            cutoff = now - self._window * 2
414	            self._requests = {
415	                ip: ts for ip, ts in self._requests.items() if ts > cutoff
416	            }
417	        return True
418	
419	    def seconds_remaining(self, client_ip: str) -> int:
420	        """Seconds until the client can make another request."""
421	        now = time.time()
422	        last = self._requests.get(client_ip, 0.0)
423	        remaining = self._window - (now - last)
424	        return max(0, int(remaining))
425

431	# Pipeline runner -- manages a single concurrent research run
432	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
433	class PipelineRunner:
434	    """Manages the lifecycle of a single research pipeline run.
435	
436	    Only one run allowed at a time. The run executes in a background
437	    asyncio task so the /api/research endpoint returns immediately.
438	    """
439	
440	    def __init__(self) -> None:
441	        self._task: Optional[asyncio.Task] = None
442	        self._status = ResearchStatus()
443	        self._lock = asyncio.Lock()
444	        self._running: bool = False
445	        self._tailer_watcher_task: Optional[asyncio.Task] = None
446	        # D2: Live steering state
447	        self._steer_directives: list[str] = []
448	        self._steer_lock = asyncio.Lock()
449	
450	    @property
451	    def running(self) -> bool:
452	        return self._task is not None and not self._task.done()
453	
454	    @property
455	    def status(self) -> ResearchStatus:
456	        s = self._status.model_copy()
457	        s.running = self.running
458	        return s
459	
460	    async def start(
461	        self,
462	        query: str,
463	        depth: str,
464	        application: str,
465	        region: str,
466	        document_ids: list[str] | None = None,
467	        research_brief: str | None = None,
468	    ) -> str:
469	        """Start a new research run. Returns the vector_id."""
470	        async with self._lock:
471	            if self.running:
472	                raise RuntimeError("A research run is already in progress")
473	
474	            # Validate depth against presets (strict -- no silent fallback)
475	            if depth not in DEPTH_PRESETS:
476	                raise ValueError(
477	                    f"Invalid depth '{depth}'. Must be one of: {list(DEPTH_PRESETS.keys())}"
478	                )
479	
480	            # Generate vector_id from timestamp
481	            ts = datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y%m%dT%H%M%S")
482	            query_hash = hashlib.sha256(query.encode()).hexdigest()[:6]
483	            vector_id = f"WEB_{ts}_{query_hash}"
484	
485	            max_iterations, max_minutes = DEPTH_PRESETS[depth]
486	
487	            self._status = ResearchStatus(
488	                running=True,
489	                vector_id=vector_id,
490	                query=query,
491	                depth=depth,
492	                started_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
493	                status="starting",
494	            )
495	
496	            self._running = True
497	            self._task = asyncio.create_task(
498	                self._run_pipeline(
499	                    vector_id=vector_id,
500	                    query=query,
501	                    application=application,
502	                    region=region,
503	                    max_iterations=max_iterations,
504	                    max_minutes=max_minutes,
505	                    document_ids=document_ids or [],
506	                    research_brief=research_brief,
507	                )
508	            )
509	
510	            return vector_id
511	
512	    async def cancel(self) -> bool:
513	        """Cancel the running research. Returns True if cancelled."""
514	        async with self._lock:
515	            if not self.running or self._task is None:
516	                return False
517	            self._task.cancel()
518	            self._status.status = "cancelled"
519	            self._running = False
520	            return True
521	
522	    async def steer(self, directive: str) -> bool:
523	        """Queue a steering directive for the running pipeline (D2)."""
524	        async with self._steer_lock:
525	            if not self.running:
526	                return False
527	            self._steer_directives.append(directive)
528	            logger.info("Steering directive queued: %s", directive[:100])
529	            return True
530	
531	    async def _run_pipeline(
532	        self,
533	        vector_id: str,
534	        query: str,
535	        application: str,
536	        region: str,
537	        max_iterations: int,
538	        max_minutes: int,
539	        document_ids: list[str] | None = None,
540	        research_brief: str | None = None,
541	    ) -> None:
542	        """Execute the research pipeline in a background task."""
543	        global _tailer, _trace_path
544	
545	        try:
546	            self._status.status = "running"
547	
548	            # Import pipeline (deferred to avoid import-time side effects)
549	            # BUG-B-102 R2e+R2g: v4 (pipeline-A-backed) is now the
550	            # production default. v1/v2/v3 remain selectable via the
551	            # explicit env flag for compatibility during migration.
552	            # v4 enforces the 5-round pipeline-A hardening (strict_verify,
553	            # corpus_approval, delimiter sanitization, unified manifest.status,
554	            # evaluator gate, tier-balanced evidence selection).
555	            graph_version = os.getenv("PG_GRAPH_VERSION", "v4")
556	            if graph_version == "v4":
557	                from src.polaris_graph.pipeline_a_ui_adapter import build_and_run_v4 as build_and_run
558	            elif graph_version == "v3":
559	                from src.polaris_graph.graph_v3 import build_and_run_v3 as build_and_run
560	            elif os.getenv("PG_V2_ENABLED", "0") == "1" or graph_version == "v2":
561	                from src.polaris_graph.graph_v2 import build_and_run
562	            elif graph_version == "v1":
563	                from src.polaris_graph.graph import build_and_run
564	            else:
565	                # Unknown version → safe default (v4, the hardened path).
566	                logger.warning(
567	                    "Unknown PG_GRAPH_VERSION=%r; falling back to v4",
568	                    graph_version,
569	                )
570	                from src.polaris_graph.pipeline_a_ui_adapter import build_and_run_v4 as build_and_run
571	
572	            # Point the trace tailer to the new trace file
573	            new_trace_path = Path(PG_LIVE_TRACE_DIR) / f"pg_trace_{vector_id}.jsonl"
574	
575	            # Clean up previous tailer watcher task if it exists
576	            if self._tailer_watcher_task is not None and not self._tailer_watcher_task.done():
577	                self._tailer_watcher_task.cancel()
578	                try:
579	                    await asyncio.shield(self._tailer_watcher_task)
580	                except (asyncio.CancelledError, Exception):
581	                    pass
582	                self._tailer_watcher_task = None
583	
584	            _trace_path = new_trace_path
585	            _tailer = TraceTailer(new_trace_path)
586	            # Start watching the new trace file
587	            self._tailer_watcher_task = asyncio.create_task(_tailer.run_watcher())
588	
589	            logger.info(
590	                "Starting research: vector_id=%s query=%r depth=%s/%d",
591	                vector_id,
592	                query[:80],
593	                max_iterations,
594	                max_minutes,
595	            )
596	
597	            # D2: Create steer_callback closure for live steering
598	            def steer_callback() -> list[str]:
599	                """Drain pending steer directives (called from _evaluate node)."""
600	                directives = list(self._steer_directives)
601	                self._steer_directives.clear()
602	                return directives
603	
604	            result = await build_and_run(
605	                vector_id=vector_id,
606	                query=query,
607	                application=application,
608	                region=region,
609	                max_iterations=max_iterations,
610	                max_execution_minutes=max_minutes,
611	                resume=False,
612	                enable_dashboard=False,  # No Rich terminal in server mode
613	                document_ids=document_ids or [],
614	                steer_callback=steer_callback,
615	                research_brief=research_brief,
616	            )
617	
618	            self._status.status = result.get("status", "completed")
619	            result_path = f"outputs/polaris_graph/{vector_id}.json"
620	            self._status.result_path = result_path
621	
622	            logger.info(
623	                "Research completed: vector_id=%s status=%s",
624	                vector_id,
625	                self._status.status,
626	            )
627	
628	        except asyncio.CancelledError:
629	            self._status.status = "cancelled"
630	            logger.info("Research cancelled: vector_id=%s", vector_id)
631	        except Exception as exc:
632	            self._status.status = "failed"
633	            self._status.error = str(exc)[:500]
634	            logger.error(
635	                "Research failed: vector_id=%s error=%s\n%s",
636	                vector_id,
637	                exc,
638	                traceback.format_exc(),
639	            )
640	        finally:
641	            self._running = False
642	
643

650	class CampaignManager:
651	    """Manages campaign lifecycle and sequential query execution.
652	
653	    Campaigns queue multiple research queries and execute them one at a time
654	    through the existing PipelineRunner. Supports snowball memory -- passing
655	    summaries from completed queries as context to subsequent queries.
656	
657	    Thread-safety: relies on asyncio single-threaded event loop.
658	    Data persisted to SQLite via campaign_store (Sprint 1, Task 1A.2).
659	    Falls back to in-memory only if campaign_store is unavailable.
660	    """

668	    async def load_persisted_campaigns(self) -> None:

693	                        ))
694	                meta = row.get("metadata_json", {})
695	                if not isinstance(meta, dict):
696	                    meta = {}
697	                campaign = CampaignData(
698	                    campaign_id=cid,

708	                    completed_count=meta.get("completed_count", 0),
709	                    failed_count=meta.get("failed_count", 0),
710	                    application=meta.get("application"),

762	    def create(self, req: CampaignRequest) -> CampaignData:
763	        """Create a new campaign from request. Returns the CampaignData."""

781	                f"No valid queries provided. Each query must be at least "
782	                f"{PG_CAMPAIGN_QUERY_MIN_LENGTH} characters."
783	            )
784	
785	        campaign = CampaignData(
786	            campaign_id=campaign_id,

788	            description=req.description,

836	    def delete(self, campaign_id: str) -> bool:
837	        """Cancel and delete a campaign. Returns True if found."""
838	        campaign = self._campaigns.get(campaign_id)
839	        if not campaign:
840	            return False
841	
842	        # Cancel if running
843	        if (
844	            self._active_campaign_id == campaign_id
845	            and self._active_task is not None
846	        ):
847	            self._active_task.cancel()
848	            self._active_campaign_id = None
849	            self._active_task = None
850	
851	        # Mark remaining queued queries as cancelled
852	        for q in campaign.queries:
853	            if q.status == "queued":
854	                q.status = "cancelled"
855	        campaign.status = "cancelled"
856	        campaign.completed_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
857	
858	        logger.info("Campaign cancelled/deleted: id=%s", campaign_id)
859	        return True

861	    async def start(self, campaign_id: str) -> None:
862	        """Start executing a campaign's queries sequentially."""
863	        campaign = self._campaigns.get(campaign_id)
864	        if not campaign:
865	            raise ValueError(f"Campaign not found: {campaign_id}")
866	
867	        if campaign.status == "running":
868	            raise RuntimeError(f"Campaign {campaign_id} is already running")
869	
870	        if self._active_campaign_id is not None:
871	            raise RuntimeError(
872	                f"Another campaign is already running: "
873	                f"{self._active_campaign_id}"
874	            )
875	
876	        campaign.status = "running"
877	        campaign.started_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
878	        self._active_campaign_id = campaign_id
879	        await self._persist_campaign(campaign)  # Sprint 1: persist on start
880	        self._active_task = asyncio.create_task(
881	            self._execute_campaign(campaign_id)
882	        )
883	
884	    async def _execute_campaign(self, campaign_id: str) -> None:
885	        """Execute all queries in a campaign sequentially with snowball memory."""
886	        campaign = self._campaigns.get(campaign_id)
887	        if not campaign:
888	            return
889	
890	        try:
891	            for idx, query_status in enumerate(campaign.queries):
892	                if query_status.status != "queued":
893	                    continue
894	
895	                # Wait for any running pipeline to finish
896	                while self._runner.running:
897	                    await asyncio.sleep(2.0)
898	
899	                query_status.status = "running"
900	                query_status.started_at = (
901	                    datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
902	                )
903	
904	                logger.info(
905	                    "Campaign %s: starting query %d/%d: %r",
906	                    campaign_id,
907	                    idx + 1,
908	                    campaign.total_queries,
909	                    query_status.query[:80],
910	                )
911	
912	                try:
913	                    vector_id = await self._runner.start(
914	                        query=query_status.query,
915	                        depth=campaign.depth,
916	                        application=query_status.application or campaign.application or "general",
917	                        region=query_status.region or "GLOBAL",
918	                        research_brief=campaign.research_brief,
919	                    )
920	                    query_status.vector_id = vector_id
921	
922	                    # Launch concurrent watcher for Campaign Map updates
923	                    watcher = asyncio.create_task(
924	                        self._watch_vector_progress(campaign, idx)
925	                    )
926	
927	                    # Wait for pipeline to complete
928	                    while self._runner.running:
929	                        await asyncio.sleep(3.0)
930	
931	                    watcher.cancel()
932	                    try:
933	                        await watcher
934	                    except asyncio.CancelledError:
935	                        pass
936	
937	                    # Check result
938	                    runner_status = self._runner.status
939	                    if runner_status.status in (
940	                        "completed",
941	                        "timeout_synthesized",
942	                    ):
943	                        query_status.status = "completed"
944	                        query_status.result_path = runner_status.result_path
945	                        campaign.completed_count += 1
946	
947	                        # Load result summary for snowball memory
948	                        self._capture_result_context(campaign, query_status)
949	                    else:
950	                        query_status.status = "failed"
951	                        query_status.error = (
952	                            runner_status.error or runner_status.status
953	                        )
954	                        campaign.failed_count += 1
955	
956	                    query_status.completed_at = (
957	                        datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
958	                    )
959	
960	                    # Copy result to campaign output directory
961	                    self._copy_result_to_campaign(campaign_id, query_status)
962	
963	                except Exception as exc:
964	                    query_status.status = "failed"
965	                    query_status.error = str(exc)[:500]
966	                    query_status.completed_at = (
967	                        datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
968	                    )
969	                    campaign.failed_count += 1
970	                    logger.error(
971	                        "Campaign %s: query %d failed: %s",
972	                        campaign_id, idx + 1, exc,
973	                    )
974	
975	                # Sprint 1: persist after each query completes/fails
976	                await self._persist_campaign(campaign)
977	
978	            # Campaign complete
979	            campaign.status = "completed"
980	            campaign.completed_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
981	            logger.info(
982	                "Campaign completed: id=%s completed=%d failed=%d",
983	                campaign_id,
984	                campaign.completed_count,
985	                campaign.failed_count,
986	            )
987	
988	        except asyncio.CancelledError:
989	            campaign.status = "cancelled"
990	            campaign.completed_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
991	            logger.info("Campaign cancelled: id=%s", campaign_id)
992	        except Exception as exc:
993	            campaign.status = "failed"
994	            campaign.completed_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
995	            logger.error(
996	                "Campaign failed: id=%s error=%s", campaign_id, exc,
997	            )
998	        finally:
999	            self._active_campaign_id = None
1000	            self._active_task = None
1001	            # Sprint 1: persist final state
1002	            if campaign:
1003	                await self._persist_campaign(campaign)

1005	    def _build_context_summary(self, campaign: CampaignData) -> str:
1006	        """Build snowball memory context from previously completed queries.
1007	
1008	        Returns a concise summary of findings from earlier queries in the
1009	        campaign, which can be used to inform subsequent queries.
1010	        """
1011	        if not campaign.campaign_context:
1012	            return ""
1013	
1014	        parts = []
1015	        for ctx in campaign.campaign_context:
1016	            query = ctx.get("query", "")
1017	            summary = ctx.get("summary", "")
1018	            source_count = ctx.get("source_count", 0)
1019	            if summary:
1020	                parts.append(
1021	                    f"Previous finding ({source_count} sources): "
1022	                    f"[{query[:100]}] {summary[:500]}"
1023	                )
1024	
1025	        return " | ".join(parts) if parts else ""
1026

1204	# TraceTailer: watches JSONL file for new events
1205	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1206	class TraceTailer:
1207	    """Tails a JSONL trace file and broadcasts new events to SSE clients.
1208	
1209	    Uses watchfiles for cross-platform (Windows-safe) file change detection.
1210	    Tracks byte offset centrally; each SSE client gets its own cursor into
1211	    the shared _all_events list so multiple browser tabs work correctly.
1212	    """
1213	
1214	    def __init__(self, trace_path: Path, poll_interval: float = 0.5):
1215	        self._path = trace_path
1216	        self._offset: int = 0
1217	        self._poll_interval = poll_interval
1218	        self._all_events: list[dict] = []
1219	        self._notify: asyncio.Event = asyncio.Event()
1220	
1221	    @property
1222	    def trace_path(self) -> Path:
1223	        """Return the trace file path (B13 diagnostic)."""
1224	        return self._path
1225	
1226	    @property
1227	    def all_events(self) -> list[dict]:
1228	        """Return all events seen so far."""
1229	        return self._all_events
1230	
1231	    def _read_new_lines(self) -> list[dict]:
1232	        """Read new bytes from the trace file and parse JSONL lines.
1233	
1234	        B13 FIX: Uses binary mode ("rb") for reliable offset tracking on
1235	        Windows. Text mode "r" translates \\r\\n to \\n, causing f.tell()
1236	        to return byte offsets that may not round-trip correctly with
1237	        f.seek(), leading to duplicate or skipped lines (event count mismatch).
1238	        """
1239	        events = []
1240	        if not self._path.exists():
1241	            return events
1242	
1243	        try:
1244	            with open(self._path, "rb") as f:
1245	                f.seek(self._offset)
1246	                new_data_bytes = f.read()
1247	                self._offset = f.tell()
1248	        except (OSError, PermissionError) as exc:
1249	            logger.warning("Trace read error: %s", exc)
1250	            return events
1251	
1252	        try:
1253	            new_data = new_data_bytes.decode("utf-8")
1254	        except UnicodeDecodeError as exc:
1255	            logger.warning("Trace decode error at offset %d: %s", self._offset, exc)
1256	            return events
1257	
1258	        for line in new_data.splitlines():
1259	            line = line.strip()
1260	            if not line:
1261	                continue
1262	            try:
1263	                ev = json.loads(line)
1264	                events.append(ev)
1265	                self._all_events.append(ev)
1266	            except json.JSONDecodeError:
1267	                logger.debug("Skipping malformed line: %s", line[:100])
1268	
1269	        if events:
1270	            self._notify.set()
1271	            self._notify.clear()
1272	
1273	        return events
1274	
1275	    async def run_watcher(self) -> None:
1276	        """Background task that reads new lines and notifies waiting clients."""
1277	        try:
1278	            from watchfiles import awatch
1279	
1280	            async for changes in awatch(
1281	                str(self._path.parent),
1282	                step=int(self._poll_interval * 1000),
1283	                rust_timeout=int(self._poll_interval * 1000 * 2),
1284	            ):
1285	                for _change_type, changed_path in changes:
1286	                    changed = Path(changed_path)
1287	                    if changed == self._path or (
1288	                        changed.suffix == ".jsonl"
1289	                        and changed.name.startswith("pg_trace_")
1290	                    ):
1291	                        self._read_new_lines()
1292	        except ImportError:
1293	            logger.warning("watchfiles not available, falling back to polling")
1294	            while True:
1295	                await asyncio.sleep(self._poll_interval)
1296	                self._read_new_lines()
1297	
1298	    async def tail(self, after: int = 0) -> AsyncGenerator[dict, None]:
1299	        """Per-client async generator with independent cursor.
1300	
1301	        Each call gets its own index into _all_events, so multiple SSE
1302	        clients receive all events independently.
1303	
1304	        Args:
1305	            after: Start cursor at this index (skip events already seen
1306	                   via snapshot). WAVE-1.6 SSE dedup.
1307	        """
1308	        cursor = after
1309	
1310	        while True:
1311	            # Yield any events the client hasn't seen yet
1312	            while cursor < len(self._all_events):
1313	                yield self._all_events[cursor]
1314	                cursor += 1
1315	
1316	            # Wait for new events (with timeout for periodic liveness)
1317	            try:
1318	                await asyncio.wait_for(self._notify.wait(), timeout=1.0)
1319	            except asyncio.TimeoutError:
1320	                pass
1321

3179	async def generate_campaign_plan(request: Request):
3180	    """AI-generate a research plan from a broad query.
3181	
3182	    Uses OpenRouterClient to decompose a broad research query into
3183	    grouped domains with specific research vectors.
3184	    """
3185	    body = await request.json()
3186	    query = body.get("query", "").strip()
3187	    depth = body.get("depth", "standard")
3188	
3189	    if not query:
3190	        raise HTTPException(status_code=422, detail="query is required")
3191	
3192	    if not _OPENROUTER_CLIENT_AVAILABLE or _openrouter_client is None:
3193	        raise HTTPException(
3194	            status_code=503,
3195	            detail="LLM client not available for plan generation",
3196	        )
3197	
3198	    system_prompt = (
3199	        "You are a research planning assistant. Given a broad research query, "
3200	        "decompose it into 2-5 thematic domains, each containing 2-6 specific "
3201	        "research vectors (precise search queries). Return valid JSON only.\n\n"
3202	        "JSON format:\n"
3203	        "{\n"
3204	        '  "title": "Plan title",\n'
3205	        '  "domains": [\n'
3206	        "    {\n"
3207	        '      "name": "Domain Name",\n'
3208	        '      "vectors": [\n'
3209	        '        {"query": "Specific research query", "type": "standard"},\n'
3210	        '        {"query": "Another query", "type": "standard"}\n'
3211	        "      ]\n"
3212	        "    }\n"
3213	        "  ]\n"
3214	        "}\n\n"
3215	        "Rules:\n"
3216	        "- Each vector query should be specific and searchable\n"
3217	        "- type is always 'standard' unless the user specifies custom\n"
3218	        "- Cover different angles/perspectives of the topic\n"
3219	        "- Avoid redundant or overlapping queries"
3220	    )
3221	
3222	    depth_hints = {
3223	        "quick": "Generate 2-3 domains with 2-3 vectors each (quick survey).",
3224	        "standard": "Generate 3-4 domains with 3-5 vectors each.",
3225	        "deep": "Generate 4-5 domains with 4-6 vectors each (comprehensive).",
3226	    }
3227	    user_prompt = (
3228	        f"Research query: {query}\n\n"
3229	        f"Depth: {depth}. {depth_hints.get(depth, depth_hints['standard'])}"
3230	    )
3231	
3232	    try:
3233	        response = await _openrouter_client.generate(
3234	            prompt=user_prompt,
3235	            system=system_prompt,
3236	            max_tokens=1024,
3237	            temperature=0.7,
3238	        )
3239	        raw = response.content.strip()
3240	
3241	        # Strip code fences if present
3242	        if raw.startswith("```"):
3243	            raw = re.sub(r"^```(?:json)?\s*", "", raw)
3244	            raw = re.sub(r"\s*```$", "", raw)
3245	
3246	        plan = json.loads(raw)
3247	
3248	        # Validate structure
3249	        if not isinstance(plan.get("domains"), list):
3250	            plan = {"title": query[:100], "domains": []}
3251	        for domain in plan.get("domains", []):
3252	            if not isinstance(domain.get("vectors"), list):
3253	                domain["vectors"] = []
3254	
3255	        # Compute estimates
3256	        total_vectors = sum(
3257	            len(d.get("vectors", []))
3258	            for d in plan.get("domains", [])
3259	        )
3260	        minutes_per_vector = {"quick": 5, "standard": 12, "deep": 25}
3261	        cost_per_vector = {"quick": 0.30, "standard": 0.80, "deep": 2.00}
3262	        plan["estimated_minutes"] = (
3263	            total_vectors * minutes_per_vector.get(depth, 12)
3264	        )
3265	        plan["estimated_cost_usd"] = round(
3266	            total_vectors * cost_per_vector.get(depth, 0.80), 2
3267	        )
3268	
3269	    except json.JSONDecodeError:
3270	        plan = {
3271	            "title": query[:100],
3272	            "domains": [{
3273	                "name": "General",
3274	                "vectors": [{"query": query, "type": "standard"}],
3275	            }],
3276	            "estimated_minutes": 12,
3277	            "estimated_cost_usd": 0.80,
3278	        }
3279	    except Exception as exc:
3280	        logger.warning("Plan generation failed: %s", str(exc)[:300])
3281	        raise HTTPException(
3282	            status_code=500,
3283	            detail=f"Plan generation failed: {str(exc)[:200]}",
3284	        )
3285	
3286	    return JSONResponse(plan)

3446	@app.post("/api/documents/upload")
3447	async def upload_document(file: UploadFile = File(...)):
3448	    """Upload and ingest a document for local RAG.
3449	
3450	    Accepts multipart file upload. Saves to a temporary file, calls
3451	    DocumentIngester.ingest(), and returns document metadata.
3452	    Max file size governed by PG_MAX_UPLOAD_SIZE_MB env var (default 100).
3453	    """
3454	    if not _DOCUMENT_INGESTER_AVAILABLE or _document_ingester is None:
3455	        raise HTTPException(
3456	            status_code=503,
3457	            detail="Document ingestion not available. Check server logs.",
3458	        )
3459	
3460	    # Validate filename exists
3461	    if not file.filename:
3462	        raise HTTPException(status_code=422, detail="No filename provided.")
3463	
3464	    # Read file content and validate size before writing to disk
3465	    file_bytes = await file.read()
3466	    size_mb = len(file_bytes) / (1024 * 1024)
3467	    if size_mb > PG_MAX_UPLOAD_SIZE_MB:
3468	        raise HTTPException(
3469	            status_code=413,
3470	            detail=(
3471	                f"File size {size_mb:.1f} MB exceeds maximum allowed "
3472	                f"{PG_MAX_UPLOAD_SIZE_MB} MB (PG_MAX_UPLOAD_SIZE_MB)."
3473	            ),
3474	        )
3475	
3476	    # Determine suffix from original filename
3477	    original_name = file.filename
3478	    suffix = Path(original_name).suffix.lower() if original_name else ""
3479	
3480	    # Write to a temp file for ingestion
3481	    tmp_dir = None
3482	    try:
3483	        tmp_dir = tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix="polaris_upload_")
3484	        safe_name = "".join(
3485	            c if (c.isalnum() or c in "._- ") else "_"
3486	            for c in original_name
3487	        ) or f"upload{suffix}"
3488	        tmp_path = Path(tmp_dir) / safe_name
3489	        tmp_path.write_bytes(file_bytes)
3490	
3491	        result = await _document_ingester.ingest(tmp_path)
3492	
3493	        logger.info(
3494	            "Document uploaded: doc_id=%s filename=%s size=%.2f MB pages=%d",
3495	            result["doc_id"],
3496	            original_name,
3497	            size_mb,
3498	            result.get("pages", 0),
3499	        )
3500	
3501	        return JSONResponse({
3502	            "doc_id": result["doc_id"],
3503	            "filename": original_name,
3504	            "size_mb": round(size_mb, 3),
3505	            "pages": result.get("pages", 0),
3506	            "content_chars": len(result.get("content", "")),
3507	            "metadata": result.get("metadata", {}),
3508	            "message": "Document uploaded and ingested successfully.",
3509	        })
3510	
3511	    except DocumentIngestionError as exc:
3512	        raise HTTPException(status_code=422, detail=str(exc))
3513	    except FileNotFoundError as exc:
3514	        raise HTTPException(status_code=422, detail=str(exc))
3515	    except Exception as exc:
3516	        logger.error(
3517	            "Document upload failed: filename=%s error=%s",
3518	            original_name, str(exc)[:300],
3519	        )
3520	        raise HTTPException(
3521	            status_code=500,
3522	            detail=f"Document ingestion failed: {str(exc)[:200]}",
3523	        )
3524	    finally:
3525	        # Clean up temp directory
3526	        if tmp_dir and Path(tmp_dir).exists():
3527	            shutil.rmtree(tmp_dir, ignore_errors=True)
3528

3719	# Stopwords for grounding check (common English words that don't indicate topic)
3720	_GROUNDING_STOPWORDS = frozenset({
3721	    "the", "and", "that", "this", "with", "from", "have", "been", "were",
3722	    "they", "their", "which", "about", "into", "more", "also", "than",

3732	def _extract_key_sentences(content: str, max_chars: int) -> str:
3733	    """Extract the most information-dense sentences from content.
3734	
3735	    Scores each sentence by presence of numbers/percentages, proper nouns,
3736	    sentence length, and positional bonuses for intro/conclusion.
3737	    Returns top-scoring sentences in original order within the char budget.
3738	
3739	    Falls back to ``content[:max_chars]`` if no sentences are found.
3740	    """
3741	    if not content or len(content) <= max_chars:
3742	        return content or ""
3743	
3744	    # Split into sentences (handles common abbreviations)
3745	    sentences = re.split(r'(?<=[.!?])\s+(?=[A-Z])', content)
3746	    if not sentences:
3747	        return content[:max_chars]
3748	
3749	    scored: list[tuple[float, int, str]] = []
3750	    total = len(sentences)
3751	
3752	    for idx, sent in enumerate(sentences):
3753	        words = sent.split()
3754	        if len(words) < 3:
3755	            continue
3756	
3757	        score = 0.0
3758	
3759	        # Numbers / percentages (+2 each, max 6)
3760	        num_count = len(re.findall(r'\d+\.?\d*%?', sent))
3761	        score += min(num_count * 2, 6)
3762	
3763	        # Proper nouns — capitalized words not at sentence start (+1 each, max 4)
3764	        proper = sum(
3765	            1 for w in words[1:]
3766	            if w[0].isupper() and w.lower() not in _GROUNDING_STOPWORDS
3767	        )
3768	        score += min(proper, 4)
3769	
3770	        # Sentence length (capped at +3)
3771	        score += min(len(words) / 10.0, 3.0)
3772	
3773	        # Intro / conclusion positional boost (×1.5)
3774	        if idx < 3 or idx >= total - 3:
3775	            score *= 1.5
3776	
3777	        # Penalize fragments (<5 words)
3778	        if len(words) < 5:
3779	            score *= 0.3
3780	
3781	        scored.append((score, idx, sent))
3782	
3783	    if not scored:
3784	        return content[:max_chars]
3785	
3786	    # Sort by score desc, pick top within budget
3787	    scored.sort(key=lambda t: t[0], reverse=True)
3788	
3789	    selected: list[tuple[int, str]] = []
3790	    budget = max_chars
3791	    for _score, idx, sent in scored:
3792	        if len(sent) + 1 > budget:
3793	            continue
3794	        selected.append((idx, sent))
3795	        budget -= len(sent) + 1  # +1 for space join
3796	
3797	    # Reconstruct in original order
3798	    selected.sort(key=lambda t: t[0])
3799	    return " ".join(s for _, s in selected) if selected else content[:max_chars]

3854

3860	    system_prompt = (

3869	        "5. Bold **conceptual terms and methods** (e.g. steered molecular "
3870	        "dynamics, ensemble validation), NOT raw numbers or data values"
3871	    )
3872	    user_prompt = (
3873	        "Below are the user's uploaded sources. Read carefully.\n\n"
3874	    )
3875	    for i, ex in enumerate(excerpts, 1):
3876	        user_prompt += (

3878	            f"{ex['content']}\n"
3879	            f"=== END SOURCE {i} ===\n\n"
3880	        )
3881	    user_prompt += (
3882	        "STEP 1 — EXTRACT: Identify 5-8 most important facts, findings, "
3883	        "and methods from the source text.\n"
3884	        "STEP 2 — SYNTHESIZE: Using ONLY those facts, write a narrative "

3901	
3902	    # --- Call LLM ---
3903	    brief_client = _brief_llm_client or _openrouter_client

3916

4648	                if in_list:
4649	                    html_lines.append(f"")
4650	                html_lines.append("
    ")
4651	                in_list = True
4652	                list_type = "ul"
4653	            text = _inline_md(stripped[2:])
4654	            html_lines.append(f"
  • {text}
    • ")
4655	            continue
4656	
4657	        # Ordered list
4658	        ol_match = re.match(r"^\d+\.\s+(.+)$", stripped)
4659	        if ol_match:
4660	            if not in_list or list_type != "ol":
4661	                if in_list:
4662	                    html_lines.append(f"")
4663	                html_lines.append("
      ")
4664	                in_list = True
4665	                list_type = "ol"
4666	            text = _inline_md(ol_match.group(1))
4667	            html_lines.append(f"
    1. {text}
      2. ")
4668	            continue
4669	
4670	        # Empty line
4671	        if not stripped:
4672	            html_lines.append("")
4673	            continue
4674	
4675	        # Paragraph
4676	        html_lines.append(f"
      {_inline_md(stripped)}
      ")
4677	
4678	    # Close any open list
4679	    if in_list:
4680	        html_lines.append(f"")
4681	
4682	    if in_code_block:
4683	        html_lines.append("
")
4684	
4685	    return "\n".join(html_lines)

4688	def _inline_md(text: str) -> str:
4689	    """Convert inline markdown (bold, italic, code, links) to HTML.
4690	
4691	    Input text is HTML-escaped first to prevent XSS, then markdown
4692	    patterns are converted.
4693	    """
4694	    text = html.escape(text)
4695	    # Bold: **text** or __text__
4696	    text = re.sub(r"\*\*(.+?)\*\*", r"\1", text)
4697	    text = re.sub(r"__(.+?)__", r"\1", text)
4698	    # Italic: *text* or _text_
4699	    text = re.sub(r"\*(.+?)\*", r"\1", text)
4700	    text = re.sub(r"(?\1", text)
4701	    # Inline code: `code`
4702	    text = re.sub(r"`(.+?)`", r"\1", text)
4703	    # Links: [text](url)
4704	    text = re.sub(r"\[(.+?)\]\((.+?)\)", r'\1', text)
4705	    # CITE markers: [CITE:xxx] -> superscript
4706	    text = re.sub(r"\[CITE:([^\]]+)\]", r'[\1]', text)
4707	    # Citation numbers: [1], [2], etc.
4708	    text = re.sub(r"\[(\d+)\]", r'[\1]', text)
4709	    return text

4716	async def _check_url_health(url: str, timeout: float = 5.0) -> dict:
4717	    """Check if a URL is accessible. Returns status dict."""
4718	    try:
4719	        async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:
4720	            async with session.head(
4721	                url,
4722	                timeout=aiohttp.ClientTimeout(total=timeout),
4723	                allow_redirects=True,
4724	            ) as resp:
4725	                return {"url": url, "status": resp.status, "alive": 200 <= resp.status < 400}
4726	    except Exception:
4727	        return {"url": url, "status": 0, "alive": False}

4730	async def _check_bibliography_urls(bibliography: list[dict], concurrency: int = 10) -> list[dict]:
4731	    """Check all bibliography URLs concurrently with a semaphore limit.
4732	
4733	    Returns list of health check results with url, status, and alive fields.
4734	    """
4735	    urls = []
4736	    for entry in bibliography:
4737	        if isinstance(entry, dict):
4738	            url = entry.get("url", "")
4739	            if url and url.startswith("http"):
4740	                urls.append(url)
4741	        elif isinstance(entry, str) and entry.startswith("http"):
4742	            urls.append(entry)
4743	
4744	    if not urls:
4745	        return []
4746	
4747	    # Deduplicate
4748	    unique_urls = list(dict.fromkeys(urls))
4749	
4750	    sem = asyncio.Semaphore(concurrency)
4751	
4752	    async def check_with_sem(u: str) -> dict:
4753	        async with sem:
4754	            return await _check_url_health(u)
4755	
4756	    results = await asyncio.gather(
4757	        *[check_with_sem(u) for u in unique_urls],
4758	        return_exceptions=True,
4759	    )
4760	
4761	    health_results = []
4762	    for r in results:
4763	        if isinstance(r, dict):
4764	            health_results.append(r)
4765	        elif isinstance(r, Exception):
4766	            health_results.append({"url": "unknown", "status": 0, "alive": False})
4767	
4768	    return health_results

5125	@app.get("/static/{filepath:path}")
5126	async def serve_static(filepath: str, request: Request):
5127	    """Serve static files with ETag/304 support for proper cache validation."""
5128	    file_path = (STATIC_DIR / filepath).resolve()
5129	    if not str(file_path).startswith(str(STATIC_DIR.resolve())):
5130	        raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail="Forbidden")
5131	    if not file_path.exists() or not file_path.is_file():
5132	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"Not found: {filepath}")
5133	
5134	    norm = filepath.replace("\\", "/")
5135	    etag_info = _STATIC_ETAGS.get(norm)
5136	    if etag_info:
5137	        client_etag = request.headers.get("if-none-match")
5138	        if client_etag and client_etag == etag_info[0]:
5139	            return Response(status_code=304, headers={"ETag": etag_info[0]})
5140	
5141	    suffix = file_path.suffix.lower()
5142	    media_types = {
5143	        ".js": "application/javascript", ".css": "text/css",
5144	        ".svg": "image/svg+xml", ".png": "image/png",
5145	        ".jpg": "image/jpeg", ".ico": "image/x-icon",
5146	        ".json": "application/json", ".woff2": "font/woff2", ".woff": "font/woff",
5147	    }
5148	
5149	    headers = {"Cache-Control": "no-cache, must-revalidate"}
5150	    if etag_info:
5151	        from email.utils import formatdate
5152	        headers["ETag"] = etag_info[0]
5153	        headers["Last-Modified"] = formatdate(etag_info[1], usegmt=True)
5154	
5155	    return FileResponse(
5156	        path=str(file_path),
5157	        media_type=media_types.get(suffix, "application/octet-stream"),
5158	        headers=headers,
5159	    )

5165	async def start_cloudflare_tunnel(port: int) -> Optional[str]:
5166	    """Spawn cloudflared quick tunnel and return the public URL.
5167	
5168	    Quick tunnels require no auth, no config. The URL is printed to stderr
5169	    by cloudflared and we parse it out.
5170	    """
5171	    try:
5172	        proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(
5173	            "cloudflared", "tunnel", "--url", f"http://localhost:{port}",
5174	            "--ha-connections", "4",
5175	            stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,
5176	            stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,
5177	        )
5178	    except FileNotFoundError:
5179	        logger.warning(
5180	            "cloudflared not found on PATH. Install from "
5181	            "https://developers.cloudflare.com/cloudflare-one/connections/"
5182	            "connect-networks/downloads/"
5183	        )
5184	        return None
5185	
5186	    url_pattern = re.compile(r"https://[a-z0-9-]+\.trycloudflare\.com")
5187	
5188	    # cloudflared prints the URL to stderr
5189	    async def read_stderr():
5190	        while True:
5191	            line = await proc.stderr.readline()
5192	            if not line:
5193	                break
5194	            text = line.decode("utf-8", errors="replace").strip()
5195	            if text:
5196	                logger.debug("[cloudflared] %s", text)
5197	            match = url_pattern.search(text)
5198	            if match:
5199	                return match.group(0)
5200	        return None
5201	
5202	    # Wait up to 30s for URL
5203	    try:
5204	        url = await asyncio.wait_for(read_stderr(), timeout=30.0)
5205	    except asyncio.TimeoutError:
5206	        logger.warning("Cloudflare tunnel timed out waiting for URL")
5207	        url = None
5208	
5209	    if url:
5210	        logger.info("=" * 60)
5211	        logger.info("CLOUDFLARE TUNNEL ACTIVE")
5212	        logger.info("Public URL: %s", url)
5213	        logger.info("=" * 60)
5214

1	"""Auto-responder for SourceAnalysisBatch requests in the loopback queue.
2	
3	Produces minimal but schema-valid responses preserving URL fidelity (critical for
4	the D3 url_to_ref test). Each source gets 2-3 atomic facts built from short
5	quoted substrings of the fetched content, with realistic tier assignments.
6	
7	Thin sources (content < 500 chars of real text, dominated by captcha / paywall
8	markers) get zero atomic facts and low relevance/quality scores.
9	
10	Handles only call_type == 'structured:SourceAnalysisBatch' prompts that begin
11	with 'Research question:' and contain 'Analyze the following'. Other requests
12	are left for the operator.
13	"""
14	from __future__ import annotations
15	
16	import json
17	import re
18	import sys
19	import time
20	from pathlib import Path
21	
22	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
23	PENDING = ROOT / "loopback" / "pending"
24	RESPONSES = ROOT / "loopback" / "responses"
25	
26	PAYWALL_PATTERNS = re.compile(
27	    r"INSUFFICIENT_CONTENT|captcha|403 (Forbidden|:)|Cloudflare|Performing security|"
28	    r"Database connection failed|Just a moment|Sign in|security verification",
29	    re.I,
30	)
31	
32	
33	def extract_short_quote(text: str, start: int = 0, max_len: int = 200) -> str:
34	    """Return a ~max_len-char substring starting near `start` that is a reasonable fact candidate."""
35	    slice_ = text[start : start + max_len + 300]
36	    # Trim to a sentence boundary if possible
37	    m = re.search(r"\.(?:\s+[A-Z]|\s+$|$)", slice_[:max_len + 200])
38	    if m:
39	        return slice_[: m.end()].strip()
40	    return slice_[:max_len].strip()
41	
42	
43	def parse_sources(prompt: str) -> list[dict]:
44	    """Split prompt into per-source blocks."""
45	    parts = re.split(r"^Source URL:\s*", prompt, flags=re.MULTILINE)
46	    sources = []
47	    for part in parts[1:]:
48	        m_url = re.match(r"(\S+)", part)
49	        if not m_url:
50	            continue
51	        url = m_url.group(1).strip()
52	        m_title = re.search(r"^Source title:\s*(.+)", part, re.MULTILINE)
53	        title = m_title.group(1).strip() if m_title else ""
54	        m_type = re.search(r"^Source type:\s*(\S+)", part, re.MULTILINE)
55	        stype = m_type.group(1).strip() if m_type else "web"
56	        m_content = re.search(r"^Content:\s*\n(.*?)(?:\n---\s*|\nSource URL:|\Z)", part, re.DOTALL | re.MULTILINE)
57	        content = m_content.group(1).strip() if m_content else ""
58	        sources.append({"url": url, "title": title, "type": stype, "content": content})
59	    return sources
60	
61	
62	def build_analysis(src: dict) -> dict:
63	    content = src["content"] or ""
64	    # Detect shell/paywall
65	    real_content = re.sub(r"<[^>]+>", "", content)  # strip html
66	    is_shell = len(real_content) < 500 or PAYWALL_PATTERNS.search(real_content[:2000]) is not None
67	    source_type_map = {
68	        "academic": "journal_article",
69	        "web": "web",
70	        "news": "news",
71	        "other": "other",
72	    }
73	    src_type = source_type_map.get(src["type"], "web")
74	
75	    if is_shell:
76	        return {
77	            "source_url": src["url"],
78	            "source_title": src["title"] or src["url"][:80],
79	            "source_type": "other",
80	            "source_quality": 0.0,
81	            "overall_relevance": 0.0,
82	            "year": 0,
83	            "authors": [],
84	            "venue": "",
85	            "doi": "",
86	            "atomic_facts": [],
87	            "evidence_summary": "INSUFFICIENT_CONTENT: paywall/shell/captcha.",
88	        }
89	
90	    # Topic-agnostic fact extraction: any sentence with quantitative/technical substance.
91	    # FIX-HALLUC-3: Broadened from IF/MetS-only to work across all 10 loopback topics
92	    # (materials, economics, climate, AI/ML, law, chemistry, biology, public health, engineering).
93	    facts = []
94	    sentences = re.split(r"(?<=[.!?])\s+(?=[A-Z])", real_content[:12000])
95	    # Quantitative signals: numbers, percentages, statistical markers, citation markers
96	    quantitative = re.compile(
97	        r"\d+\s*(%|kg|mg|mm|cm|km|nm|ppm|ppb|GHz|MHz|Hz|GPa|MPa|kPa|Pa|°C|K|J|eV|keV|MeV|"
98	        r"mol|mmol|μmol|dL|mL|L|g|mcg|IU|bp|kb|Mb|Gb|USD|EUR|years?|months?|weeks?|days?|hours?|"
99	        r"participants|patients|adults|subjects|cases|events|sites|samples|measurements)|"
100	        r"\b(SMD|MD|OR|RR|HR|CI|SD|SE|SEM|95%|99%|p\s*[=<>]|n\s*=|r\s*=|β\s*=|R²|R\^2|ICC|AUC|"
101	        r"RMSE|MAE|MSE|F1|IC50|EC50|Kd|Km|pH)\b|"
102	        r"\b(19|20)\d{2}\b|"  # years (1900s-2000s)
103	        r"\b\d+\.\d+\b",  # decimals
104	        re.I,
105	    )
106	    # Technical-entity signals: named entities, technical terminology
107	    technical = re.compile(
108	        r"\b(meta-analysis|systematic review|RCT|randomized|cohort|longitudinal|cross-sectional|"
109	        r"catalyst|Faradaic|electrolyte|adhesion|fatigue|yield|tensile|modulus|crystalline|"
110	        r"permafrost|carbon|emission|RCP|IPCC|scenario|mitigation|adaptation|"
111	        r"RLHF|alignment|transformer|attention|embedding|fine-tuning|"
112	        r"regulation|directive|article|clause|compliance|obligation|"
113	        r"tau|prion|neuron|synaptic|propagation|aggregation|"
114	        r"obesity|prevalence|intervention|LMIC|income|country|"
115	        r"fiber optic|sensor|bridge|steel|corrosion|inspection|monitoring|"
116	        r"Regulation \d+|Directive \d+|ISO \d+|ASTM|IEC|IEEE \d+)\b",
117	        re.I,
118	    )
119	    relevance_base = 0.55
120	    confidence_base = 0.88
121	    for s in sentences:
122	        s = s.strip()
123	        if not s or len(s) < 40 or len(s) > 400:
124	            continue
125	        has_quant = bool(quantitative.search(s))
126	        has_tech = bool(technical.search(s))
127	        if has_quant or has_tech:
128	            q = s[:250]
129	            statement = q if len(q) < 140 else q[:140].rsplit(" ", 1)[0] + "..."
130	            facts.append({
131	                "statement": statement,
132	                "direct_quote": q,
133	                "fact_category": "statistic" if has_quant else "causal_link",
134	                "relevance_score": relevance_base + (0.15 if has_quant and has_tech else 0.0),
135	                "confidence": confidence_base,
136	                "perspective": "Scientific",
137	                "entities": [],
138	            })
139	        if len(facts) >= 3:
140	            break
141	
142	    if not facts:
143	        # Fall back to first meaningful sentence
144	        for s in sentences:
145	            s = s.strip()
146	            if 40 <= len(s) <= 300:
147	                facts.append({
148	                    "statement": s[:140] + ("..." if len(s) > 140 else ""),
149	                    "direct_quote": s[:250],
150	                    "fact_category": "named_entity",
151	                    "relevance_score": 0.4,
152	                    "confidence": 0.8,
153	                    "perspective": "Scientific",
154	                    "entities": [],
155	                })
156	                break
157	
158	    return {
159	        "source_url": src["url"],
160	        "source_title": src["title"] or src["url"][:80],
161	        "source_type": "journal_article" if src_type == "academic" else src_type,
162	        "source_quality": 0.7,
163	        "overall_relevance": 0.65,
164	        "year": 0,
165	        "authors": [],
166	        "venue": "",
167	        "doi": "",
168	        "atomic_facts": facts,
169	        "evidence_summary": f"Auto-extracted {len(facts)} facts from {src['title'][:60]}.",
170	    }
171	
172	
173	def try_handle(req_path: Path) -> bool:
174	    try:
175	        with req_path.open(encoding="utf-8") as f:
176	            req = json.load(f)
177	    except Exception:
178	        return False
179	    if req.get("call_type", "") != "structured:SourceAnalysisBatch":
180	        return False
181	    prompt = req.get("prompt", "") or ""
182	    if "Analyze the following" not in prompt:
183	        return False
184	    sources = parse_sources(prompt)
185	    if not sources:
186	        return False
187	    analyses = [build_analysis(s) for s in sources]
188	    result = {"analyses": analyses}
189	    req_id = req.get("request_id") or req_path.stem.replace("req_", "")
190	    resp_path = RESPONSES / f"resp_{req_id}.json"
191	    tmp = resp_path.with_suffix(".tmp")
192	    total_facts = sum(len(a["atomic_facts"]) for a in analyses)
193	    with tmp.open("w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
194	        json.dump(
195	            {"content": json.dumps(result, ensure_ascii=False),
196	             "input_tokens": len(prompt) // 4,
197	             "output_tokens": total_facts * 60 + 100},
198	            f,
199	            ensure_ascii=False,
200	        )
201	    tmp.replace(resp_path)
202	    print(f"  [auto-ANALYZE] {req_path.name} -> {len(analyses)} sources, {total_facts} facts total")
203	    return True
204	
205	
206	def main() -> int:
207	    handled_total = 0
208	    idle_polls = 0
209	    MAX_IDLE_POLLS = 1800
210	    while True:
211	        handled_this_cycle = 0
212	        for p in sorted(PENDING.glob("req_*.json")):
213	            try:
214	                if try_handle(p):
215	                    handled_this_cycle += 1
216	                    handled_total += 1
217	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
218	                print(f"  [auto-ANALYZE] error on {p.name}: {exc}")
219	        if handled_this_cycle == 0:
220	            idle_polls += 1
221	            if idle_polls >= MAX_IDLE_POLLS:
222	                break
223	            time.sleep(1.0)
224	        else:
225	            idle_polls = 0
226	            time.sleep(0.5)
227	    print(f"[auto-ANALYZE] drained {handled_total} source-analysis batches")
228	    return 0
229	
230	
231	if __name__ == "__main__":
232	    sys.exit(main())

1	"""Auto-responder for repetitive GRADE certainty rating calls in the loopback queue.
2	
3	Only handles the specific pattern of reason() calls whose prompt starts with
4	'Assign GRADE certainty ratings'. All other pending requests are left alone
5	for the operator to handle.
6	
7	Heuristic: map each evidence item's tier + content signal to a GRADE rating:
8	- GOLD + (systematic review | meta-analysis | RCT | SMD | MD | 95% CI) -> HIGH
9	- GOLD otherwise -> MODERATE
10	- SILVER + (RCT | meta-analysis | systematic review) -> MODERATE
11	- SILVER otherwise -> LOW
12	- BRONZE -> LOW
13	- (captcha | INSUFFICIENT_CONTENT | 403 | binary) -> VERY_LOW
14	
15	Writes responses to loopback/responses/resp_<id>.json with the plain text rating list
16	that the pipeline parses. Exits when no auto-serve-able request remains for one poll cycle.
17	"""
18	from __future__ import annotations
19	
20	import json
21	import re
22	import sys
23	import time
24	from pathlib import Path
25	
26	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
27	PENDING = ROOT / "loopback" / "pending"
28	RESPONSES = ROOT / "loopback" / "responses"
29	DONE = ROOT / "loopback" / "done"
30	
31	STATS_SIGNAL = re.compile(r"\bSMD\b|\bMD\b|\b95%\s*CI\b|\bmeta-analysis\b|\bsystematic review\b|\bRCT\b|\brandomized\b", re.I)
32	DOWNGRADE_SIGNAL = re.compile(r"INSUFFICIENT_CONTENT|captcha|403|forbidden|paywall|binary|PDF binary", re.I)
33	
34	
35	def rate_item(tier: str, statement: str) -> str:
36	    stmt = statement or ""
37	    if DOWNGRADE_SIGNAL.search(stmt):
38	        return "VERY_LOW"
39	    has_stats = bool(STATS_SIGNAL.search(stmt))
40	    if tier == "GOLD":
41	        return "HIGH" if has_stats else "MODERATE"
42	    if tier == "SILVER":
43	        return "MODERATE" if has_stats else "LOW"
44	    if tier == "BRONZE":
45	        return "LOW"
46	    return "LOW"
47	
48	
49	def try_handle(req_path: Path) -> bool:
50	    try:
51	        with req_path.open(encoding="utf-8") as f:
52	            req = json.load(f)
53	    except Exception:
54	        return False
55	    prompt = req.get("prompt", "") or ""
56	    if not prompt.startswith("Assign GRADE certainty ratings"):
57	        return False
58	    items = re.findall(
59	        r"^(\d+)\.\s*\[(\w+)\][^|]+\|\s*Statement:\s*([^\n]+)",
60	        prompt,
61	        re.MULTILINE,
62	    )
63	    if not items:
64	        return False
65	    lines = [f"{num}. {rate_item(tier, stmt)}" for num, tier, stmt in items]
66	    content = "\n".join(lines)
67	    req_id = req.get("request_id") or req_path.stem.replace("req_", "")
68	    resp_path = RESPONSES / f"resp_{req_id}.json"
69	    tmp = resp_path.with_suffix(".tmp")
70	    with tmp.open("w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
71	        json.dump(
72	            {"content": content, "input_tokens": 500, "output_tokens": 40},
73	            f,
74	            ensure_ascii=False,
75	        )
76	    tmp.replace(resp_path)
77	    print(f"  [auto-GRADE] {req_path.name} -> {len(items)} ratings")
78	    return True
79	
80	
81	def main() -> int:
82	    handled_total = 0
83	    idle_polls = 0
84	    # Persistent mode: exit only after many idle polls so operator can run this in background
85	    # and let it drain GRADE calls while they work other things.
86	    MAX_IDLE_POLLS = 1800  # ~5 minutes at 1s poll
87	    while True:
88	        handled_this_cycle = 0
89	        for p in sorted(PENDING.glob("req_*.json")):
90	            try:
91	                if try_handle(p):
92	                    handled_this_cycle += 1
93	                    handled_total += 1
94	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
95	                print(f"  [auto-GRADE] error on {p.name}: {exc}")
96	        if handled_this_cycle == 0:
97	            idle_polls += 1
98	            if idle_polls >= MAX_IDLE_POLLS:
99	                break
100	            time.sleep(1.0)
101	        else:
102	            idle_polls = 0
103	            time.sleep(0.5)
104	    print(f"[auto-GRADE] drained {handled_total} GRADE requests; exiting after {MAX_IDLE_POLLS}s idle.")
105	    return 0
106	
107	
108	if __name__ == "__main__":
109	    sys.exit(main())
110

1	"""Auto-responder for PageSummaryBatch requests in the loopback queue.
2	
3	The agentic search loop asks for per-page research notes (url, title, summary,
4	perspectives, key_facts, knowledge_contribution) across 6 pages per batch.
5	For loopback validation, quality isn't the goal — pipeline progression is.
6	
7	Heuristic extraction:
8	- Parse prompt by "--- PAGE: <title> ---" + "URL: <url>" + "CONTENT:" blocks
9	- summary: first 3 substantive sentences (>=40 chars, <400 chars) = ~150-200 words
10	- key_facts: sentences with numbers/percentages/CI/SMD/technical terms (up to 5)
11	- perspectives: defaults to ["Scientific"] (agentic search derives perspective tags elsewhere)
12	- knowledge_contribution: one short sentence derived from the first fact
13	
14	Handles only call_type == 'structured:PageSummaryBatch'. Other requests left alone.
15	"""
16	from __future__ import annotations
17	
18	import json
19	import re
20	import sys
21	import time
22	from pathlib import Path
23	
24	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
25	PENDING = ROOT / "loopback" / "pending"
26	RESPONSES = ROOT / "loopback" / "responses"
27	
28	PAYWALL_PATTERNS = re.compile(
29	    r"INSUFFICIENT_CONTENT|captcha|403 (Forbidden|:)|Cloudflare|Performing security|"
30	    r"Database connection failed|Just a moment|Sign in|security verification",
31	    re.I,
32	)
33	
34	QUANT_SIGNAL = re.compile(
35	    r"\d+\s*(%|kg|mg|mmHg|nm|ppm|GPa|MPa|mol|mmol|mL|L|g|IU|bp|years?|months?|weeks?|days?|hours?|participants|patients|adults|subjects)|"
36	    r"\b(SMD|MD|OR|RR|HR|CI|SD|95%|99%|p\s*[=<>]|n\s*=|r\s*=|R²)\b|"
37	    r"\b(19|20)\d{2}\b|"
38	    r"\b\d+\.\d+\b",
39	    re.I,
40	)
41	
42	
43	def parse_pages(prompt: str) -> list[dict]:
44	    """Split the prompt into per-page blocks: {url, title, content}."""
45	    # Split on page delimiter
46	    parts = re.split(r"^--- PAGE:\s*(.+?)\s*---\s*$", prompt, flags=re.MULTILINE)
47	    pages = []
48	    # parts[0] is preamble; then title, block, title, block, ...
49	    for i in range(1, len(parts), 2):
50	        title = parts[i].strip() if i < len(parts) else ""
51	        body = parts[i + 1] if i + 1 < len(parts) else ""
52	        m_url = re.search(r"^URL:\s*(\S+)", body, re.MULTILINE)
53	        url = m_url.group(1).strip() if m_url else ""
54	        m_content = re.search(r"^CONTENT:\s*\n(.*?)(?:\n---\s*PAGE:|\Z)", body, re.DOTALL | re.MULTILINE)
55	        content = m_content.group(1).strip() if m_content else body
56	        pages.append({"url": url, "title": title, "content": content})
57	    return pages
58	
59	
60	def build_note(page: dict) -> dict:
61	    content = page.get("content") or ""
62	    url = page.get("url") or ""
63	    title = page.get("title") or url[:80]
64	
65	    real_content = re.sub(r"<[^>]+>", "", content)
66	    is_shell = len(real_content) < 400 or PAYWALL_PATTERNS.search(real_content[:2000]) is not None
67	
68	    if is_shell:
69	        return {
70	            "url": url,
71	            "title": title,
72	            "summary": "INSUFFICIENT_CONTENT: page appears to be paywall/captcha/shell.",
73	            "perspectives": [],
74	            "key_facts": [],
75	            "knowledge_contribution": "",
76	        }
77	
78	    sentences = re.split(r"(?<=[.!?])\s+(?=[A-Z])", real_content[:10000])
79	    cleaned = [s.strip() for s in sentences if 40 <= len(s.strip()) <= 400]
80	
81	    # Summary: first 3 substantive sentences, combined.
82	    summary_parts = cleaned[:3]
83	    summary = " ".join(summary_parts)[:1500]
84	
85	    # Key facts: sentences with quantitative signal.
86	    facts = []
87	    for s in cleaned:
88	        if QUANT_SIGNAL.search(s):
89	            facts.append(s[:250])
90	        if len(facts) >= 5:
91	            break
92	    # If no quantitative sentences, fall back to 2-3 cleaned ones.
93	    if not facts:
94	        facts = [s[:250] for s in cleaned[:3]]
95	
96	    contribution = (
97	        f"Adds per-page evidence on the research topic: {facts[0][:140]}"
98	        if facts else "Provides background context."
99	    )
100	
101	    return {
102	        "url": url,
103	        "title": title[:200],
104	        "summary": summary,
105	        "perspectives": ["Scientific"],
106	        "key_facts": facts,
107	        "knowledge_contribution": contribution,
108	    }
109	
110	
111	def try_handle(req_path: Path) -> bool:
112	    try:
113	        with req_path.open(encoding="utf-8") as f:
114	            req = json.load(f)
115	    except Exception:
116	        return False
117	    if req.get("call_type", "") != "structured:PageSummaryBatch":
118	        return False
119	    prompt = req.get("prompt", "") or ""
120	    pages = parse_pages(prompt)
121	    if not pages:
122	        return False
123	    notes = [build_note(p) for p in pages]
124	    result = {"notes": notes}
125	    req_id = req.get("request_id") or req_path.stem.replace("req_", "")
126	    resp_path = RESPONSES / f"resp_{req_id}.json"
127	    tmp = resp_path.with_suffix(".tmp")
128	    with tmp.open("w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
129	        json.dump(
130	            {"content": json.dumps(result, ensure_ascii=False),
131	             "input_tokens": len(prompt) // 4,
132	             "output_tokens": len(notes) * 100 + 50},
133	            f,
134	            ensure_ascii=False,
135	        )
136	    tmp.replace(resp_path)
137	    total_facts = sum(len(n["key_facts"]) for n in notes)
138	    print(f"  [auto-PAGESUMMARY] {req_path.name} -> {len(notes)} pages, {total_facts} facts total")
139	    return True
140	
141	
142	def main() -> int:
143	    handled_total = 0
144	    idle_polls = 0
145	    MAX_IDLE_POLLS = 1800
146	    while True:
147	        handled_this_cycle = 0
148	        for p in sorted(PENDING.glob("req_*.json")):
149	            try:
150	                if try_handle(p):
151	                    handled_this_cycle += 1
152	                    handled_total += 1
153	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
154	                print(f"  [auto-PAGESUMMARY] error on {p.name}: {exc}")
155	        if handled_this_cycle == 0:
156	            idle_polls += 1
157	            if idle_polls >= MAX_IDLE_POLLS:
158	                break
159	            time.sleep(1.0)
160	        else:
161	            idle_polls = 0
162	            time.sleep(0.5)
163	    print(f"[auto-PAGESUMMARY] drained {handled_total} page-summary batches")
164	    return 0
165	
166	
167	if __name__ == "__main__":
168	    sys.exit(main())
